name: color-tracr
description: Highly performant, ultra-lightweight color marking extension for the editor.

colorTracr.enable.markdownDescription:
Language IDs where Color Tracr marks colors.

- Use `*` to enable it for every language.
- Prepend `!` to a language ID to exclude it (e.g., `["*", "!markdown"]`).
- Leave the list empty to disable the extension everywhere.

colorTracr.excludePaths.markdownDescription:
Glob patterns for files and folders to skip while scanning the workspace for color definitions.

This only affects the background scan that resolves variables (`var(--…)`, `$…`, `@…`) and Tailwind classes across files. Colors inside the files you open are always marked, regardless of this setting.

colorTracr.markerType.markdownDescription:
How each detected color is marked.

- `highlight` – Highlight the color's text with the color itself.
- `dot-before` – Render a colored dot before the color.
- `dot-after` – Render a colored dot after the color.

colorTracr.showAlpha.markdownDescription:
Render each marker using the color's parsed transparency.

When disabled, markers are always drawn fully opaque and the alpha channel is ignored.

colorTracr.markNamedColors.markdownDescription:
Mark CSS named colors such as `red`, `tomato`, etc..

Applies only to CSS and CSS-embedding languages (HTML, Vue, Svelte, …), and skips names that are part of a larger token (e.g., the `red` in `text-red`).

colorTracr.markVariables.markdownDescription:
Mark CSS custom properties (`var(--name)`), SCSS variables (`$name`), and LESS variables (`@name`) with the color they resolve to.

Definitions are resolved across the whole workspace, so a variable is still marked when it is defined in a different (already scanned) file.

Accepts a list of language IDs – use `*` for all, or prepend `!` to exclude a language.

colorTracr.markTailwind.markdownDescription:
Mark Tailwind CSS color utility classes such as `bg-red-500` or `text-primary`, including arbitrary values (`bg-[#0af]`) and opacity modifiers (`bg-red-500/50`).

Accepts a list of language IDs – use `*` for all, or prepend `!` to exclude a language.

colorTracr.matchRgbWithNoFunction.markdownDescription:
Mark raw RGB channels that are not wrapped in `rgb()` (e.g., `255, 0, 0` or `255 0 0`).

Only integer channels are matched; values containing `%` are ignored.

Accepts a list of language IDs – use `*` for all, or prepend `!` to exclude a language.

colorTracr.matchHslWithNoFunction.markdownDescription:
Mark raw HSL values that are not wrapped in `hsl()` (e.g., `255, 100%, 50%`).

The saturation and lightness must keep their `%` sign so they are not mistaken for plain numbers.

Accepts a list of language IDs – use `*` for all, or prepend `!` to exclude a language.

colorTracr.matchLchWithNoFunction.markdownDescription:
Mark raw LCH values that are not wrapped in `lch()` (e.g., `60% 40 25`).

Accepts a list of language IDs – use `*` for all, or prepend `!` to exclude a language.

colorTracr.matchOklchWithNoFunction.markdownDescription:
Mark raw OKLCH values that are not wrapped in `oklch()` (e.g., `60% 0.15 25`).

Accepts a list of language IDs – use `*` for all, or prepend `!` to exclude a language.

colorTracr.useARGB.markdownDescription:
Interpret alpha-containing hex codes as ARGB instead of RGBA – i.e. `#AARRGGBB` and `#ARGB` rather than `#RRGGBBAA` and `#RGBA`.

This also re-evaluates hex colors reported by other color providers so they follow the same channel order.

Accepts a list of language IDs – use `*` for all, or prepend `!` to exclude a language.
